title: Cozy Algerian Avocado Tagine
description: A garlicky and flaky Algerian dish featuring avocado hass pulp, slow cooked to perfection, suitable for a Buddhist temple gathering.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large clay or ceramic tagine over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the chickpeas and stir to combine.
Add the diced avocado and stir gently to combine.
Cover the tagine and transfer it to the oven, where it will cook at 300 degrees Fahrenheit for 30 minutes.
Remove the tagine from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
